function.name: read
function.description: Read the contents of a file. Supports text files and images (jpg, png, gif, webp). Images are sent as attachments. For text files, output is truncated to 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Use offset/limit for large files. When you need the full file, continue with offset until complete.

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

function.name: bash
function.description: Execute a bash command in the current working directory. Returns stdout and stderr. Output is truncated to last 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). If truncated, full output is saved to a temp file. Optionally provide a timeout in seconds.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds (optional, no default timeout)

function.name: edit
function.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

oldText.description: Exact text for one targeted replacement. It must be unique in the original file and must not overlap with any other edits[].oldText in the same call.

newText.description: Replacement text for this targeted edit.

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

function.name: write
function.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does. Automatically creates parent directories.

path.description: Path to the file to write (relative or absolute)

content.description: Content to write to the file

function.name: subagent
function.description:
Delegate to subagents or manage agent definitions.

EXECUTION (use exactly ONE mode):
• Before executing, use { action: "list" } to inspect configured agents/chains. Only execute agents listed as executable/non-disabled.
• SINGLE: { agent, task? } - one task; omit task for self-contained agents
• CHAIN: { chain: [{agent:"agent-a"}, {parallel:[{agent:"agent-b",count:3}]}] } - sequential pipeline with optional parallel fan-out
• PARALLEL: { tasks: [{agent,task,count?,output?,reads?,progress?}, ...], concurrency?: number, worktree?: true } - concurrent execution (worktree: isolate each task in a git worktree)
• Optional context: { context: "fresh" | "fork" } (default: if any requested agent has defaultContext: "fork", the whole invocation uses fork; otherwise "fresh"; inspect agent defaults via { action: "list" })
• Goal-style requests: when the user says “/goal”, “goal”, “active goal”, “work until evidence says done”, or “verify against a goal”, model that as explicit acceptance. Use acceptance.criteria for the target, acceptance.evidence/verify for proof, acceptance.stopRules for constraints, and acceptance.maxFinalizationTurns for the bounded loop.

CHAIN TEMPLATE VARIABLES (use in task strings):
• {task} - The original task/request from the user
• {previous} - Text response from the previous step (empty for first step)
• {chain_dir} - Shared directory for chain files (e.g., <tmpdir>/pi-subagents-<scope>/chain-runs/abc123/)

Example: { chain: [{agent:"agent-a", task:"Analyze {task}"}, {agent:"agent-b", task:"Plan based on {previous}"}] }

MANAGEMENT (use action field, omit agent/task/chain/tasks):
• { action: "list" } - discover executable agents/chains
• { action: "get", agent: "name" } - full detail; packaged agents use dotted runtime names like "package.agent"
• { action: "create", config: { name: "custom-agent", package: "code-analysis", systemPrompt, systemPromptMode, inheritProjectContext, inheritSkills, defaultContext, ... } }
• { action: "update", agent: "code-analysis.custom-agent", config: { package: "analysis", ... } } - merge
• { action: "delete", agent: "code-analysis.custom-agent" }
• Use chainName for chain operations; packaged chains also use dotted runtime names

CONTROL:
• { action: "status", id: "..." } - inspect an async/background run by id or prefix
• { action: "interrupt", id?: "..." } - soft-interrupt the current child turn and leave the run paused
• { action: "resume", id: "...", message: "...", index?: 0 } - follow up with a live async child or revive a completed async/foreground child from its session

DIAGNOSTICS:
• { action: "doctor" } - read-only report for runtime paths, discovery, sessions, and intercom

agent.description: Agent name (SINGLE mode) or target for management get/update/delete

task.description: Task (SINGLE mode, optional for self-contained agents)

action.description: Management/control action. Omit for execution mode.

id.description: Run id or prefix for action='status', action='interrupt', or action='resume'.

runId.description: Target run ID for action='interrupt' or action='resume'. Defaults to the most recently active controllable run for interrupt. Prefer id for new calls.

dir.description: Async run directory for action='status' or action='resume'.

index.description: Zero-based child index for actions that target a specific child.

message.description: Follow-up message for action='resume'. Use index to choose a child from multi-child runs.

chainName.description: Chain name for get/update/delete management actions

config.description: Agent or chain config for create/update. Agent: name, package (optional namespace; runtime name becomes package.name), description, scope ('user'|'project', default 'user'), systemPrompt, systemPromptMode, inheritProjectContext, inheritSkills, defaultContext ('fresh'|'fork'), model, tools (comma-separated), extensions (comma-separated), skills (comma-separated), thinking, output, reads, progress, maxSubagentDepth. Chain: name, package, description, scope, steps (array of {agent, task?, output?, outputMode?, reads?, model?, skill?, progress?}). Presence of 'steps' creates a chain instead of an agent. String values must be valid JSON.

count.description: Repeat this parallel task N times with the same settings.

output.description: Output filename/path (string), or false to disable file output

outputMode.description: Return saved output inline (default) or only a concise file reference. file-only requires output to be a path.

reads.description: Files to read before running (array of filenames), or false to disable

progress.description: Enable progress.md tracking for this task

skill.description: Skill name(s) to inject (comma-separated), array of strings, or boolean (false disables, true uses default)

acceptance.description: Optional acceptance contract. Use this for goal-style requests such as /goal, goal, active goal, or work until evidence says done: criteria define the target, evidence/verify define proof, stopRules define constraints, and maxFinalizationTurns defines the bounded loop. When present, the child must complete a same-session self-review/repair loop before acceptance is evaluated.

tasks.description: PARALLEL mode: [{agent, task, count?, output?, outputMode?, reads?, progress?}, ...]

concurrency.description: Top-level PARALLEL mode only: max concurrent tasks. Defaults to config.parallel.concurrency or 4.

worktree.description: Create isolated git worktrees for each parallel task. Prevents filesystem conflicts. Requires clean git state. Per-worktree diffs included in output.

task.description: Task template with variables: {task}=original request, {previous}=prior step's text response, {chain_dir}=shared folder, {outputs.name}=prior named output. Required for first step, defaults to '{previous}' for subsequent steps.

phase.description: Optional phase/group label for status and graph rendering.

label.description: Optional user-facing label for this chain step.

as.description: Optional safe identifier used as {outputs.name} in later chain steps.

outputSchema.description: JSON Schema object for strict structured output. Non-object roots are rejected.

output.description: Output filename/path (string), or false to disable file output

outputMode.description: Return saved output inline (default) or only a concise file reference. file-only requires output to be a path.

reads.description: Files to read before running (array of filenames), or false to disable

skill.description: Skill name(s) to inject (comma-separated), array of strings, or boolean (false disables, true uses default)

acceptance.description: Optional acceptance contract. Use this for goal-style requests such as /goal, goal, active goal, or work until evidence says done: criteria define the target, evidence/verify define proof, stopRules define constraints, and maxFinalizationTurns defines the bounded loop. When present, the child must complete a same-session self-review/repair loop before acceptance is evaluated.

task.description: Task template with {task}, {previous}, {chain_dir} variables. Defaults to {previous}.

phase.description: Optional phase/group label for status and graph rendering.

label.description: Optional user-facing label for this parallel task.

as.description: Optional safe identifier used as {outputs.name} in later chain steps.

outputSchema.description: JSON Schema object for strict structured output. Non-object roots are rejected.

count.description: Repeat this parallel task N times with the same settings.

output.description: Output filename/path (string), or false to disable file output

outputMode.description: Return saved output inline (default) or only a concise file reference. file-only requires output to be a path.

reads.description: Files to read before running (array of filenames), or false to disable

skill.description: Skill name(s) to inject (comma-separated), array of strings, or boolean (false disables, true uses default)

acceptance.description: Optional acceptance contract. Use this for goal-style requests such as /goal, goal, active goal, or work until evidence says done: criteria define the target, evidence/verify define proof, stopRules define constraints, and maxFinalizationTurns defines the bounded loop. When present, the child must complete a same-session self-review/repair loop before acceptance is evaluated.

phase.description: Optional phase/group label for status and graph rendering.

label.description: Optional user-facing label; item templates are supported.

outputSchema.description: JSON Schema object for strict structured output. Non-object roots are rejected.

output.description: Output filename/path (string), or false to disable file output

outputMode.description: Return saved output inline (default) or only a concise file reference. file-only requires output to be a path.

reads.description: Files to read before running (array of filenames), or false to disable

skill.description: Skill name(s) to inject (comma-separated), array of strings, or boolean (false disables, true uses default)

acceptance.description: Optional acceptance contract. Use this for goal-style requests such as /goal, goal, active goal, or work until evidence says done: criteria define the target, evidence/verify define proof, stopRules define constraints, and maxFinalizationTurns defines the bounded loop. When present, the child must complete a same-session self-review/repair loop before acceptance is evaluated.

parallel.description: Static parallel tasks array, or a single dynamic fanout child template when expand/collect are present.

output.description: Prior named structured output to expand from.

path.description: JSON Pointer into the structured output, e.g. /items.

item.description: Template variable name for each item. Defaults to item.

key.description: JSON Pointer relative to each item for stable child ids.

maxItems.description: Required fanout bound unless configured globally.

onEmpty.description: Empty input behavior. Defaults to skip.

as.description: Safe output name for the ordered collected result array.

outputSchema.description: JSON Schema object for strict structured output. Non-object roots are rejected.

worktree.description: Create isolated git worktrees for each parallel task.

items.description: Chain step: use {agent, task?, ...} for sequential, {parallel: [...]} for static concurrent execution, or {expand, parallel: {...}, collect} for dynamic fanout.

chain.description: CHAIN mode: sequential pipeline where each step's response becomes {previous} for the next. Use {task}, {previous}, {chain_dir} in task templates.

context.description: 'fresh' or 'fork' to branch from parent session. If omitted, any requested agent with defaultContext: 'fork' makes the whole invocation forked; otherwise the default is 'fresh'.

chainDir.description: Persistent directory for chain artifacts. Default: a user-scoped temp directory under <tmpdir>/ (auto-cleaned after 24h)

async.description: Run in background (default: false, or per config)

agentScope.description: Agent discovery scope: 'user', 'project', or 'both' (default: 'both'; project wins on name collisions)

includeProgress.description: Include full progress in result (default: false)

share.description: Upload session to GitHub Gist for sharing (default: false)

sessionDir.description: Directory to store session logs (default: temp; enables sessions even if share=false)

clarify.description: Show TUI to preview/edit before execution. Explicit clarify: true keeps the run foreground for the clarify UI; omitted clarify can still run in the background when async: true is set.

enabled.description: Enable/disable subagent control attention tracking for this run

needsAttentionAfterMs.description: No-observed-activity window before a run needs attention

activeNoticeAfterMs.description: Active-long-running notice threshold by elapsed ms (default: 240000)

activeNoticeAfterTurns.description: Optional active-long-running notice threshold by assistant turns (disabled by default)

activeNoticeAfterTokens.description: Optional active-long-running notice threshold by total tokens (disabled by default)

failedToolAttemptsBeforeAttention.description: Consecutive mutating-tool failures before escalating to needs_attention (default: 3)

notifyOn.description: Control event types that should notify the parent/orchestrator. Defaults to active_long_running and needs_attention.

notifyChannels.description: Notification channels to use when available. Defaults to event, async, and intercom.

output.description: Output file for single agent (string), or false to disable. Relative paths resolve against cwd.

outputMode.description: Return saved output inline (default) or only a concise file reference. file-only requires output to be a path.

skill.description: Skill name(s) to inject (comma-separated), array of strings, or boolean (false disables, true uses default)

acceptance.description: Optional acceptance contract. Use this for goal-style requests such as /goal, goal, active goal, or work until evidence says done: criteria define the target, evidence/verify define proof, stopRules define constraints, and maxFinalizationTurns defines the bounded loop. When present, the child must complete a same-session self-review/repair loop before acceptance is evaluated.
